narrative: For a company whose product IS a 275M-contact PII database, DLP value isn't 'flag every email' — it's bulk-exfil detection + precision. Open on a bulk contact-DB export to a personal Drive (their 2018 breach in miniature), then the infra secret that would hand over the whole database; then the precision inversion (stay quiet on routine business-contact product data, catch a genuine personal-mobile leak); round out with ingested customer-CRM custody and the HR layer.

findings.finding: AI-native B2B sales-intelligence + engagement platform (contact database + sequences + dialer + pipeline). Founded 2015, ~1,604 employees, ~16,000 customer teams.

Core asset is a database of 230–275M+ B2B contacts across ~73M accounts, 65+ data points each (name, work email, phone, title, company), ~97% email accuracy. Their deliverable is personal contact data at scale.
A 2018 breach exposed ~125.9M unique emails / 200M contact records (name, email, phone, company, title) straight from the prospect database — the canonical 'bulk contact-DB exfil' nightmare.
GDPR & CCPA are central — Apollo operates as both Data Processor and Controller, offers CCPA opt-out of sale/sharing, and faced GDPR scrutiny after the breach.

Contact data flows out by design — CSV exports, API pulls, CRM (Salesforce/HubSpot) sync, sequences, dialer; Apollo's own SDRs/AEs dogfood the product, so reps hold large exports and prospect-filled sequences.
findings.source: inference from product surfaces + GTM model

hypotheses.hypothesis: Bulk contact-DB exfil — a rep or integration dumping a large contact list (emails/phones/names at scale) to CSV/personal drive is the crown-jewel risk; the signal is volume + destination, not one record.
hypotheses.basis: Their 2018 breach was exactly this (125.9M emails from the prospect DB); product is bulk PII that exports constantly.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Legitimate business-contact data (work emails/phones/names in normal product context) must NOT be flagged — over-flagging is unusable alert fatigue; the value is distinguishing product data from a genuine leak.
hypotheses.basis: The product IS contact PII; every system is saturated with it by design — the precision inversion.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Ingested customer CRM data (synced Salesforce/HubSpot) is third-party PII under Apollo's custody; leaking it breaches customer trust.
hypotheses.basis: Enrichment/sync pulls customers' CRM data into Apollo.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Contact-DB / enrichment / platform secrets (DB connection strings, scraper & API keys) are critical — one leaked key = access to the 275M-contact database itself.
hypotheses.basis: A data platform of this scale runs on credentialed pipelines + a large API.

decisions.rationale: Apollo's product is PII, so the differentiated DLP story is bulk-exfil + 'don't flag our product data', not single-record detection.
decisions.drove: the bulk contact_export carrier + the in-product fp_near_miss carrier as the two lead pieces
7 carriers across 6 surfaces, ~2 dozen indexed plants

decisions.choice: GitHub, CI, Google Drive, Apollo Platform, Salesforce CRM, Slack
decisions.rationale: Where contact data, secrets, and ingested CRM data actually live; the Apollo product itself is a surface (it holds the business-contact data).

decisions.choice: Data Platform Eng, Platform Eng, SDR/AE (dogfooders), Customer Success, HR
decisions.rationale: Defining persona is the SDR/AE who lives in the product — protagonist of the export/sequence carriers, not a generic engineer.
decisions.drove: bulk export + Slack carriers attributed to reps; secrets to data-platform eng; CRM custody to CS

decisions.choice: routine business-contact data, internal record IDs, UUID sequence ids, placeholder keys
decisions.rationale: The work-product inversion: their deliverable looks like the thing to catch, so the precision near-misses ARE ordinary contact data.
decisions.drove: the in-product results carrier tagged fp_near_miss + ID/UUID/placeholder near-misses

decisions.choice: personal mobile in Slack prose, spaced/dashed phone, base64/line-split keys, a sensitive record buried in a bulk product file
decisions.rationale: Recall on the shapes a whitespace-fragile detector misses, plus needle-in-product-data.
decisions.drove: tagged fn_evasive plants in Slack + CI + the export

open_questions: Exact infra stack (cloud, DB, scraper tooling) assumed — would refine which secret types to plant.
Whether to model 'personal vs business' PII split more explicitly (consumer emails/mobiles in the DB) — raises GDPR stakes; kept light for now.
Document carriers (.xlsx/.pdf) not included — text/CSV/JSON only.
